admissionRequirementsRtf:
Admission requirements

For information on admission requirements, refer to the Faculty of Science.
description: This program is offered by the School of Earth and Ocean Sciences in the Faculty of Science. In addition to the requirements below, students must consult their Faculty program requirements.
programNotes:
Program notes

  • BIOL 490D, EOS 490 or 1.5 units of 400-level MRNE coursework offered through the Bamfield Marine Sciences Centre may be used in lieu of one of BIOL 319...PHYS 441 if an appropriate oceans-related topic is chosen, and with permission of the school.
  • Only one Minor may be declared on any degree program.
programRequirements:
• Complete all of the following
    • Complete all of:
      • EOS120 - The Dynamic Earth (1.5)
    • Complete 1 of:
      • EOS110 - Oceans and Atmosphere (1.5)
      • EOS340 - Atmospheric Physics (1.5)
      • EOS365 - Climate and Society (1.5)
      • PHYS340 - Atmospheric Physics (1.5)
    • Complete all of:
      • EOS311 - Biological Oceanography (1.5)
      • EOS312 - Introductory Chemical Oceanography (1.5)
      • EOS313 - Introductory Geological Oceanography (1.5)
      • EOS314 - Descriptive Physical Oceanography (1.5)
      • EOS401 - Oceanographic Field School (1.5)
    • Complete 1 of:
      • BIOL319 - Marine Ecology (1.5)
      • BIOL335 - Ichthyology (1.5)
      • BIOL461 - Fisheries Ecology and Management (1.5)
      • BIOL466 - Frontiers in Marine Biology (1.5)
      • EOS403 - Global Biogeochemical Cycles (1.5)
      • EOS408 - Marine Geology (1.5)
      • EOS410 - Global Tectonics (1.5)
      • EOS425 - Aqueous Chemistry in the Earth and Ocean (1.5)
      • EOS431 - Physical Oceanography (1.5)
      • EOS433 - The Physics of Climate (1.5)
      • PHYS426 - Fluid Mechanics (1.5)
      • PHYS441 - Physical Oceanography (1.5)
programRequirementsRtf:
Program requirements

A student may declare a Minor in Ocean Sciences when enrolled in an Honours, Major, or General Program offered by SEOS or another department or school (which need not be in the Faculty of Science).

Students are encouraged to consult the School’s Ocean Science Mentor before admission to the Ocean Science Minor Program at the end of the first or second year of their undergraduate program.
title: Ocean Sciences
